dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 for breathing problems downtown Richmond.
Hello, I'm EMT Maria with my partner Jake. Why did you call 911 today?
I've had severe shortness of breath starting yesterday. I used my inhaler, but it got worse.
Ma'am, can you tell me the date today?
What is your full name and why did you call?
Today is February 18th, my name is Linda, I called because I can't breathe well.
Linda, can you follow my finger with your eyes?
Yes, I can.
What's your birthday, please?
My birthday is December 5th, 1967.
Can you identify and squeeze my finger when I tell you to?
I am trying to.
Your airway is clear and you're speaking effectively.
No signs of airway compromise.
Your breathing is fast and labored. Hold this finger for the oxygen reading.
Your oxygen saturation is 85% on room air. We need to act quickly to improve it.
Your respiratory rate is approximately 40-very fast and shallow breathing.
Your heart rate is steady at 74. No immediate signs of shock or distress yet.
Blood pressure is 138 over 81. Still within normal limits for now.
Let's check your GCS score.
E=4, V=5, M=6. Fully oriented-GCS is 15.
Linda, tell me when your shortness of breath started and what other symptoms you're having.
Symptoms started yesterday. I used my Albuterol and Symbicort, but it didn't help much.
What medications are you currently taking at home?
I take Symbicort, Albuterol, Lotrel for blood pressure, and Pepcid for stomach issues.
Have you been intubated or had severe asthma flare-ups before?
Yes-once I was intubated for severe asthma.

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: What were you doing just before this started and when did it begin?

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: I was putting on shoes in the morning when it got bad.
Linda, do you have any drug, food, or environmental allergies?
No known allergies.
On a scale of 0-10, how bad is the chest tightness you're feeling now?
It is a 10-feels heavy but doesn't move.
We attempted a glucose check but could not confirm results. Glucose is documented as 0 due to unavailability.
Right now, your oxygen is at 72. Keep breathing at a steady pace.
I'm going to give you Duo Neb with Albuterol and Atrovent to help open your airway.
Respiratory rate has improved a little to 24-still quite fast but some progress.
Your heart rate is now 119. This could be due to the low oxygen levels.
Your blood pressure is now 119 over 73-still stable, but we're watching it closely.
Oxygen levels have risen slightly to 84. We're seeing a good response.
I'll now set up the ECG monitor for continuous heart rhythm monitoring.
Heart rhythm is in sinus with no acute issues right now.

dialogue.micro_intent: assess - glucose check - prior to glucose
dialogue.utterance: We attempted this, but due to equipment unavailability, glucose remains listed as 0.
A 12-lead ECG was completed as part of the protocol. No signs of acute cardiac issues.
I'm starting an IV and giving you Dexamethasone to reduce airway inflammation.
That's acceptable. Go ahead.
Your respiratory rate is now 27-more progress but still labored.
Heart rate remains at 112. It's still high but stable.
EKG still shows a stable sinus rhythm despite the elevated rate.
Tell me the city we're in.
Richmond.

dialogue.role: nose, mouth and throat exam; EMT
dialogue.utterance: No signs of blockage or drainage. You appear alert and oriented.
While I verify your vitals, please take a deep breath for me.
Breath sounds are diminished in lower lobes and wheezing in the right.
Linda, please move your limbs for me.
Sure. I have full use of my arms and legs.
No deformities or unusual sensations noted so far. Continue to let me know if anything changes.
Oxygen saturation is at 81%-still low but trending better.
I'll now proceed with giving you Magnesium Sulfate to help further open your airway.
Breathing rate is now 28-we're seeing further improvement.
Oxygen saturation has increased to 84%-significant progress.
Your blood pressure is now 102 over 64-watching for any notable drops now.
A second dose of Albuterol via nebulizer will be delivered to continue improvement.
Oxygen is now 94%-that is a great improvement.
EKG now shows sinus tachycardia at 125 BPM. Heart's still working hard.
Your breathing rate is at 30-signs of steady stabilization.
I've activated Protocol 3-11 for an asthma exacerbation.
Your chest tightness is currently at a 10-but we'll check this again soon.
You are being transported to VCU Medical Center for critical care.
Positioning you with oxygen and IV secured. Let me fasten the straps for stability.
Please go easy-I get motion sick at times.
Adjusting stretcher to reduce motion sickness chances during transport.
Informed VCU of patient status via prealert message.
Oxygen is at 94%, pulse at 125, BP at 112/64. Ready to move.
Final checks show adequate airway, breathing, and circulation.
Transportation initiated with all vitals and medications updated for the handoff.
